recent_calls.summary: Michayla called to report a large leak above the hot water heater inside her store and requested emergency plumbing service. The agent documented the issue and escalated it as urgent but could not dispatch a plumber after hours. Michayla then canceled the request, and the agent confirmed the cancellation and closed the call politely.

The user, Jin Suh, called to schedule an emergency service for their AC unit which is running but not cooling, causing discomfort. The agent collected all necessary details and informed Jin that the service team will contact them on the next business day to confirm the appointment, prioritizing the request due to the discomfort. The call ended with no further questions or instructions from the user.

The user inquired about whether their membership covers a summer or spring cooling checkup. The agent informed the user that coverage depends on the specific plan and noted the request for a follow-up contact to clarify the membership details. The user declined to schedule an appointment and requested a callback for more information.
The call was very brief, with the agent greeting the user but no further conversation occurred before the user hung up.

The user, Dan, called to request a status update on a heating and cooling service request. The agent, Evie, documented the request and informed Dan that a team member would follow up on the next business day. The call ended with no further requests from Dan.

Gregory called to schedule a regular service appointment for his cooling system, which is not cooling properly. The agent noted his preference for an early Monday appointment and confirmed the service address. The team will follow up next business day to confirm the appointment time.

The user called to make an emergency appointment for their AC unit under a home warranty. The agent collected all necessary details including the dispatch number and confirmed the appointment for the next day. The user expressed interest in home maintenance program information and the call ended positively with no issues.
The user requested to speak to a live agent, and the initial agent transferred the call to another agent successfully. The call lasted 15 seconds and ended with a call transfer.
The user, Betty, initiated a call and was quickly transferred to a live agent for further assistance. The agent attempted to connect Betty with a representative to address her needs.

The user called to inquire about having someone check their air conditioner and ductwork and asked about potential charges related to text messages from Budget Heating and Cooling. The agent explained that data rates might apply for text messages and transferred the call to the billing team for further assistance.

The user called to schedule a cooling service related to their American Home Shield home warranty. The agent attempted to gather more details but the user requested to speak with someone else, leading to a transfer to a live agent for further assistance.

The user called to inquire about the location of their air conditioning unit, confirming it is outside. The user had already submitted a work request and did not need further assistance. The agent acknowledged the information and offered additional help, which the user declined.
The user returned a call from Budget and was promptly transferred to a live agent for further assistance. The initial agent facilitated the transfer without any issues.

The user called to schedule a service appointment for heating and cooling maintenance, specifically to check the Freon in their air conditioner. The agent gathered preferred days and times for the appointment and requested the dispatch number. The call ended with the user being transferred to the billing team for credit-related questions.

The user called to report an ongoing issue after a technician's visit. The agent acknowledged the problem and assured the user that the team would be notified to send someone back out. The call ended shortly after without further resolution.

Glen called to schedule a preventative maintenance check for his home's cooling system. The AI assistant successfully booked an appointment for May 19th at 2 PM and noted Glen's interest in renewing his Budget Savings Club membership, which was forwarded for follow-up. Glen provided feedback and requested to speak with a live agent, leading to a successful call transfer.

The user called to verify an air conditioning service appointment scheduled for Monday, May 18th at 8 AM. The agent could not find the appointment in the system and offered to transfer the user to a live representative, which the user accepted. The call was successfully transferred to a live agent for further assistance.
The user requested to speak to a representative, and the agent promptly transferred the call to a live agent. The transfer was successful, and the call ended shortly after.

The user, Patricia, called to speak with someone about scheduling and making a payment. The agent, Sophie, informed her that she could not access billing information directly and transferred the call to the billing team for assistance.
The agent greeted the user and attempted to connect her with a live agent for further assistance. The call was then successfully transferred to another number.
The agent initiated the call and requested the user's service address ZIP code, but the user hung up immediately after the greeting without providing any information.

The user called to discuss an air conditioning issue under an active warranty claim and wanted to talk about a replacement quote after a technician visit. The agent gathered necessary details and then transferred the call to a live representative as requested by the user.

The user returned a call from Budget and was quickly transferred to a live agent for further assistance. The initial agent acknowledged the user's intent and facilitated the transfer without any issues.
The call was very brief, with the agent greeting the user and asking how to assist, but the user hung up immediately without responding.

Aaron Spencer from the Center for Autism Education called to report a rooftop HVAC unit not cooling in room twelve. The agent confirmed the correct service address and details, and informed Aaron that someone from Budget Heating, Cooling & Plumbing would contact him shortly. Aaron declined information about maintenance programs and feedback requests.

The user called to schedule a routine air conditioning checkup under their American Home Shield home warranty plan for June 10th in the afternoon. The agent confirmed the details and informed the user that someone from Budget Heating, Cooling & Plumbing would contact them shortly. The user declined information about maintenance programs and feedback requests.

The user called to check on their maintenance agreement coverage. The agent was unable to provide full details but transferred the call to a live agent for further assistance. The user expressed interest in confirming coverage and potentially obtaining a new maintenance agreement if not covered.
The user called Budget and requested to speak to a live agent after being hung up on. The agent apologized and successfully transferred the call to another live agent.
The user called to make a payment, but the agent did not have direct access to billing information and transferred the call to the billing team for assistance.
The user called and was quickly transferred to a live agent for customer service assistance. The initial agent, Sophie, did not engage further but facilitated the transfer promptly.

The user called to inquire about whether refrigerant was added to their AC during a recent technician visit or if a new appointment needed to be scheduled. The agent acknowledged the issue and transferred the call to a live agent for further assistance.

The user called back to follow up on a home warranty claim and to set up an appointment after missing a call from the company. The agent explained that appointments are handled differently and transferred the user to a live agent for further assistance.

The user, Dr. Meyer, called to ask a question about using a tankless water heater system at their Innsbruck property. The agent confirmed that Budget installs and services tankless systems and assured the user that their question would be passed to Richard, the serviceman, who would follow up directly. The user provided their phone number for Richard to contact them.

The user identified themselves as being with First American Home Warranty and declined to provide a ZIP code. They requested to speak with an office representative, and the agent successfully transferred the call to a live agent.
The call was very brief, with the agent introducing herself but no further conversation occurred before the user hung up.
The user called customer service and was promptly transferred to a live agent for further assistance. The initial agent, Sophie, facilitated the transfer without any issues.
The user called back and was quickly transferred to a live agent for further assistance. The initial agent, Sophie, facilitated the transfer without engaging in a detailed conversation.
The user called to make a payment, but the agent did not have direct access to billing information and transferred the call to the billing team for assistance.
The call was very brief, with the agent greeting the user and asking how to assist, but the user hung up immediately without responding.
The user attempted to speak with a representative, and the agent transferred the call to a live agent for further assistance. The transfer was successful, and the call ended shortly after.
The user called to verify if someone had called them earlier. The agent attempted to transfer the call to a live agent for further assistance, but the user hung up shortly after.

The user called to request a quote for replacing an HVAC flu and was transferred to a live agent for further assistance. The initial agent confirmed the user's request and initiated the transfer successfully.
The user called customer service and was promptly transferred to a live agent for further assistance. The initial agent facilitated the transfer without any issues.

Albert Washington called to check on an existing appointment scheduled for Saturday, but the agent was unable to find the appointment in the system. After multiple confirmations, Albert requested to be connected to a live representative for further assistance. The call was successfully transferred to a live agent.
The call was very brief and the agent attempted to engage the user, but the user did not respond and hung up shortly after.
The call was a brief interaction where the agent attempted to connect the user, Jennifer, with a live representative. The call ended with a successful transfer to another agent.
The user requested to speak to a real person, and the agent promptly transferred the call to a live agent. The transfer was successful, and the call ended shortly after.

John Henson called to inquire about the arrival time of his technician for an appointment. The agent confirmed the appointment details and informed John that a team member would follow up with an update. The call ended after confirming the contact information and appointment time.
The user requested to speak to a live agent, and the call was promptly transferred to another agent for further assistance.

The user, Jen from American Home Shield, called to provide details for a dispatch involving Ken Hunter, including a request to adjust the out-of-pocket cost for a line set replacement. The agent took down the information and assured that the team would follow up on the next business day. The call ended with no further requests from the user.
